highlights.text: My general lean is to defer to the trend.

My lean ES could spend some time consolidating and resting after this rally, before continuing up I wrote Friday at 4pm: “After a large rally when the parabolic phase ends (it hasn’t yet) price typically needs to digest the move and this is done either in time (price consolidates in a wide range) or in price (direct selloff).
In reality, there is only one thing that ever causes a rally or violent squeeze in ES: My core setup, The Failed Breakdown.
Failed Breakdowns are the footprint of institutional accumulation and occur when ES goes elevator down and flushes a low.
Red days like this don’t come for free in ES and every elevator down sell resolves with a violent squeeze when a Failed Breakdown triggers.
I was looking for more upside for the rest of the week, but we would need another Failed Breakdown to propel it.
On Tuesday at 9:40AM Tuesday, we flushed 6701 (there was a big shelf of lows there set on Monday/early Tuesday) to 6675, then recovered 6701 for a Failed Breakdown.
In today’s newsletter I’ll talk this, I’ll do a deep dive into the Failed Breakdown we had last Tuesday of 6701 that gave us a clean 160 point leg up.

post_title: After 5 Green Days In A Row, SPX Takes A Breather. Reset For More Upside, Or Top? Dec 2nd Plan
